verses.text: Le Seigneur a créé et gouverne maintenant la terre et tout ce qu'elle contient (voir **1Co 10.25-26** ). Il prend compte de tous ses habitants ( **Ps 33.14** ), quel que soit leur statut ( **49.2-3** ).
Dieu a tout créé avec ordre et stabilité (voir Ps **89.12 ** ; **102.26 ** ; **104.5** ).

Ces questions invitent le lecteur à détourner son regard des méchants et à s'examiner lui-même (voir **15.1** ). • Le lieu saint de Dieu est la montagne sainte, en référence à la fois à Jérusalem et au ciel.

Ceux qui ont des mains pures n'ont pas versé de sang ni blessé au sein de relations (voir **9.13** ; **15.2-5** ). • Ceux qui ont un cœur pur s'engagent pleinement envers le Seigneur, comme en témoignent leur force de caractère, leur transparence et leur dévotion ( **51.12** ; **73.1** ; voir **Mt 5.8** ).
Les citoyens du royaume de Dieu comprennent tous ceux qui le cherchent. Le Seigneur les invite à entrer en sa présence.
Les citoyens du royaume de Dieu comprennent tous ceux qui le cherchent. Le Seigneur les invite à entrer en sa présence.

24:7 Lift up your heads, O ye gates,.... By which the gates of hell are not meant; nor are the words to be understood of the descent of Christ thither, to fetch the souls of Old Testament saints from thence; who the Papists dream were detained in an apartment there, as in a prison, called by them "limbus patrum"; seeing these, immediately upon their separation from the body, were in a state of happiness and glory, as the parable of the rich man and Lazarus shows;

24:8 Who is this King of glory?.... Which question is put by the church, or particular believers; not through ignorance, as the daughters of Jerusalem, Sol 5:9; or the Pharisees, when Christ made his public entrance into Jerusalem, Matt 21:10; much less in pride and haughtiness, in scorn and derision, as Pharaoh, Exo 5:1; and the Capernaites, John 6:42; but as wondering at the glories and excellencies of his person, and as desirous of knowing more of him. The answer to the question is, the Lord strong and mighty: he whose name alone is Jehovah;

24:9 Lift up your heads, O ye gates even lift them up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in. See Gill on Ps 24:7. This is repeated on account of the backwardness and negligence of churches, and particular believers, to open and let Christ in; as may be seen in the case of the church in Sol 5:2; as well as the more to set forth the greatness and glory of Christ, about to make his entrance, and to command a proper awe and reverence of him: some think respect is had to the twofold coming of Christ;
L'Éternel des armées règne depuis sa demeure, le Temple à Jérusalem ( **84.4-6** ). Il rejette le péché et les pécheurs ( **24.3-6** ), mais protège tous ceux qui habitent Sion.
